name: Rand Paul
subject: More crucial than ever
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Dear Patriot:

2016 is shaping up to be a year more
important than I had ever imagined.

From the vacancy in the Supreme Court, to
protecting the 2nd Amendment from the Obama Administration, to
preventing a possible Clinton administration, we've got our work
cut out for us.

And building a strong liberty presence in the
Senate is becoming more and more crucial.

That's why I need your support today to help
me win in Kentucky and get us one step closer to maintaining a
much-needed GOP majority in the fall.

My team put together an end-of-quarter
fundraising blitz to help get us through the next steps in the
campaign. If you can, watch my video to hear a little more about
why this deadline is so important.

We really have so much at stake.

Can you imagine what Hillary Clinton could do
with a Democrat majority? Can you imagine that disaster?

Now is really the time to come together to
ensure a better future for our country.

A big fundraising turnout this quarter can
help us build momentum and send a message to the Washington
machine that no matter who wins the White House, the Senate will
be standing strong for liberty.

I hope you can stand with me to protect our
freedoms by joining our end-of-quarter goals today.

In Liberty,
Rand Paul

We can't let Toomey get away with partisan political games that prevent the U.S. Senate from doing their job to support hardworking families. **We have to keep up the pressure -- and that 's exactly what I'm going to do with your help.

name: Jeb Bush
subject: It's Time to Get Behind Ted Cruz
body:
Friend -- I wanted you to be the first to know that today I am endorsing Ted Cruz for President.

Ted is a consistent, principled conservative who has demonstrated the ability to appeal to voters and win primary contests, including yesterday's Utah caucus.

As I said from the moment I launched my presidential campaign, the stakes in 2016 couldn't be higher.

Washington is broken, too many families are stuck in poverty and Western civilization is under attack from radical Islamic terrorists, as evidenced by the horrific attack in Brussels, which was preceded by attacks in Paris and California.

For the sake of our party and country, we must overcome the divisiveness and vulgarity Donald Trump has brought into the political arena or we will certainly lose our chance to defeat the Democratic nominee, most likely Hillary Clinton, this fall.

That is the only way we can reverse President Obama's failed domestic and foreign policy agenda, and turn our country around.

Republicans can win back the White House and put our nation on a path to security and prosperity if we support a nominee who can unite our party and articulate how conservative policies will help people rise up and reach their full potential.

I hope you will join me in supporting Ted's campaign by visiting [**www.tedcruz.org**](http://click1.email.jeb2016.com/wjvqkwjmzsgbfhfdbwyqzbthjjbtmhkmqwnwgmhwjfwd_olrwwmqf.html), and contributing and volunteering.

Onward,

Jeb

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: Two more BIG victories last night!
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
We just scored two more victories ... big ones, this time in Utah and Idaho.
Here's why this matters: **we walked away from the three states that voted yesterday with a majority of the delegates.** That's yuge. That's how we win the nomination.
We always knew last Tuesday was the high water mark for the Clinton campaign. **Now it 's our turn to start winning large majorities in many of the remaining states** until we take the pledged delegate lead on June 7.
Now we all need to start thinking about what's next: the 142 delegates at stake in Washington, Alaska, and Hawaii this Saturday.
[**Make a $3 contribution to our campaign today and we 're going to cut into Hillary Clinton's lead even further with a significant number of delegates in Washington this weekend.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/idaho-and-utah?refcode=em160322-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/idaho-and-utah?refcode=em160322-full")
Bernie drew a huge crowd in Boise, Idaho on Monday and another 14,000 people to a rally in Salt Lake City, Utah last week. And last night we found out that we are actually leading in a general election poll against Donald Trump in Utah.
It isn't often that a Democratic candidate for president has a chance to carry Utah, but our political revolution is attracting significant support from Democrats, Independents, and even some Republicans. That's part of the reason we're the best chance for Democrats to retain the White House, and to enact transformational change after the election.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

name: Corry Bliss
subject: There's a real choice in Ohio's Senate race
body:
| Peter -- Since we kicked off the general election last week, the campaign has released two web ads detailing the records of Ted Strickland and Rob Portman.

In "Mixed and Spotty," even Ted Strickland admits he has a bad record. Under his watch, Ohio lost more than 350,000 jobs and ranked 48th in job creation. Despite being in Congress for a decade, he failed to pass a single bill into law -- and was named as one of the most ineffective and absent members of Congress.

Meanwhile, Rob is fighting for Ohio jobs. "Defending Ohio Jobs" tells the story of ArmorSource, a military combat helmet manufacturer in Hebron. ArmorSource’s state-of-the-art helmets weigh less than their competitors' helmets and are less expensive to make – but their competitors lobbied Congress to keep existing contracts without a fair and open bidding process. They contacted Rob and he took up the fight to secure more than 200 Ohio jobs.

[](http://www.robportman.com/defending_ohio_jobs_vs_mixed_and_spotty?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=1)
In this election, Ohio voters will have a clear choice between moving forward with Rob's pro-growth policies for higher wages and more jobs, or a return to Ted Strickland’s failed policies of higher taxes, burdensome regulations, and bigger government.

[Please watch our new ads and send them to 5 of your friends](http://www.robportman.com/defending_ohio_jobs_vs_mixed_and_spotty?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=2)! Sincerely,

Corry Bliss
Campaign Manager [](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/donate?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=3) [](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=4)[](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=5)[](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/donate?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=dojvmas_blash&n=6 "Donate Here!")

body:
Dear Patriot:

We have so much at stake in 2016.

It's not just about the White House -- it's
so much more.

We have candidates on both sides of the aisle
advocating for more foreign aid and interference in the Middle
East.

We have a potential presidential candidate
threatening to put the coal industry out of business.

We have a Supreme Court seat up for grabs --
and a Supreme Court nominee who threatens our 2nd Amendment
rights.

We need a voice for the Constitution standing
strong in the Senate now more than ever.

We know Rand is that voice.

We've seen him fight for our freedoms these
past six years, and we know we can trust him to do it again.

As we approach the end of this quarter, we're
up against a tight fundraising deadline.

We've set up an end of quarter goal of
raising $200,000 this week to get Rand back to the Senate for
another term. Can you help us reach our goals?

If you haven't had a chance yet, check out
Rand's latest video explaining just how important this is.

We have to show the Washington machine that
the liberty vote is alive and well -- and we still
#StandWithRand.

Join us today.

In liberty,
Doug Stafford

name: Joe Sestak
subject: The Strategy for Terror: Past is Prologue
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194fc/3476a20e/c7e0112/27458425/3647910813/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

Peter, I wanted you to see the below statement that I sent to press just a few minutes ago that includes my thoughts on the strategy for defeating ISIS -- and securing the peace.
* * *
On 9/11, I walked out of the Pentagon shortly before the plane hit. Returning, I look down the hill at the destruction and immediately helped establish a Navy crisis response center nearby. I arrived home in the early morning hours, and upon returning again, I was directed by the Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) to establish the Navy's anti-terrorism unit, Deep Blue. A few days later, the CNO took the single power point slide I gave him to the "tank," where the Joint Chiefs of Staff were meeting with then-Secretary of Defense Rumsfeld. My slide cautioned that this new war with terror would last "at least a decade." My assignment took me to Afghanistan early in the conflict. There for a brief mission, I met up with SEALS late at night, watched detainees brought in....and learned that the illiteracy rate of women in Afghanistan was 98%.
I then commanded a carrier battle group for the air strikes into the country - and later Iraq. As General Powell said of Iraq, "if you break it, you own it." And we broke it. Today, I often reflect upon my time at the National Security Council at the White House overseeing the Clinton's Administration's annual update of our nation's National Security Strategy of Engagement, laying out how to secure America's peace and prosperity, protect our interests abroad and at home, and grow our values - without entanglement.
This week's horrific terror strike in Brussels, Belgium, highlighted once again that this war on terror is on a timeline far beyond what I even imagined when I wrote the original time frame for our military leaders. But, if followed with greater adherence, the national security principles of what we must do to defeat the threat of international terrorism - including ISIS - remain resilient to win the war as well as to secure an enduring peace. First, in a global war, no nation stands alone. I saw this firsthand after 9/11, when I commanded a battle group in the Persian Gulf. I had 30 ships to carry out our mission - but only 10 of them were American ships. We were a coalition, and success was defined by our ability to work seamlessly with foreign militaries. It's how we won the Cold War, with over 60 defense arrangements with other nations, as well as U.S. leadership of international diplomatic and economic institutions. This need is starkly apparent today. We must work more closely with our global allies to share intelligence, support police actions against terror suspects, run bombing raids, and conduct joint on-the-ground operations to capture and kill ISIS members in Syria and in homelands.
In an increasingly complex world, this requires occasionally working with those who are adversarial to us, such as Russia and Iran. Unscrupulous nations often liaise with unscrupulous elements. But when these adversarial nations are aligned against a common threat - ISIS - we need the intelligence on and from their elements, and their arms aimed at our shared enemy. Second, and in conjunction, diplomacy matters. Look through the Wiki-leaks and see how resourceful in intelligence gathering, alliance building, spying, and advancing U.S. interests the State Department is. The downgrading of their manpower and resources has hindered our efforts at a time when information sharing is our most-needed asset.
Diplomacy isn't meant to be a cup of tea. It is often intended to "hurt" if the diplomatic overture isn't accepted (e.g., Iranian and Iraqi sanctions). Diplomacy is used to benefit another nation's interest - if we gain by doing so. So let President Assad remain in Syria (for now) if his supporters (Iran and Russia) then pivot to focus on the common enemy, ISIS. In return, we restrain the more moderate anti-Assad militias with Saudi and Jordanian support, corralling together other Arab nations, with disjointed "partners" from Iran and Russia, to Turkey and Iraq. Diplomacy must work even with the devil....just don't sin. Third, economics is the bedrock of power - ours and our adversaries'. It's why China wants to dominate the South China Sea: to secure the undersea resources that it needs for economic power projection. And it's why the ISIS caliphate must be denied the approximate $2 billion in annual revenue flow from oil field profits to taxes, ransom to theft. Our airpower must more aggressively kick the legs out from under these economic support systems that fuel ISIS' foreign policy (such as destroying oil fields and not just oil tankers) and cutting off internal funding by destroying the infrastructure that collects/extorts taxes from the population. Our Treasury and international banking institutions we lead need to close down the remaining money to choke ISIS to death. Fourth, the new domain of warfare is cyberspace. We must dominate this new area of warfare as we do any other, so that we know what ISIS is thinking, trying and doing. With troubling questions about the competence of Belgium's and other allies' intelligence and law enforcement services, we must enhance our support for their efforts in order to protect ourselves. The right metric of warfare today is knowledge gained by sensors, human intelligence and the network, and our capability to quickly turn this gained information into swift action. Measuring what it takes to win our conflicts requires leadership that shifts the procurement benchmark away from capacity in numbers of ships, brigades, air wings, and toward capability in acquiring knowledge.
But it also takes leadership to address a resolvable conflict between national security, civil rights and proprietary rights. Appropriate safeguards that also permit access to dangerous information that flows through the internet and into consumer phones must be established. Whether it's Apple's proprietary rights or our citizens' civil rights on the internet, resolution of the proper balance cannot be permitted to wait for the next crisis. Resolve them now ... or our security may be breached before we do. Finally, don't just win the war, but secure the peace - or, as General Powell said, we will still "own" the aftermath of unresolved conflict in the Middle East. Military strategists cautioned centuries ago that, in war, leaders must not "take the first step without considering the last." U.S. strategy to defeat ISIS needs an explanation of how we want our military and diplomatic efforts to end. For once the Caliphate ends, where do remaining ISIS forces lurk off to? Who fills the cracks left by the Caliphate's breakage? How is Syria rebuilt after 9 million of its people have been displaced, 250,000 killed, and more maimed? What of the replacement of basic services and infrastructure in the region, from homes to rudimentary sanitary and water services? What conflict do these open areas provide fertile ground for?
What is the ending we want with the demise of ISIS that precludes us from having to return? Simply, although imperfect, it is a peace that provides us stability, which likely means Russia's continued influence in Syria; Iran with its limited role in the outcome; Sunnis and Shias separated if they cannot peacefully co-exist; and the Kurds no more restive than a semi-autonomous state. That is why the 98% illiteracy rate of Afghani women I learned about when on the ground at the beginning of the war struck me most: resolving that issue would do more to secure the peace than most else.
After all, while our military stopped the problem of a fascist Germany, it took the Marshall Plan of economic recovery to secure the peace by a democratic Germany with economic security. As I reminisce on the time from the plane striking the Pentagon on 9/11 to today's effort to stop ISIS - an outgrowth of Al-Qaida in Iraq that arose after our invasion of Iraq - I acknowledge that the fog of war means no strategy is perfect.
But today, the fog can be lifted by better utilization of the light that cyberspace can shine; the intelligence sharing that other nations and groups can provide; a diplomacy that binds others to our common interests; and a strategy that has as it's ultimate objective not only winning, but securing the peace for our security and prosperity. As John F. Kennedy said, "The hour is late, but the agenda is long," and we must be persistent in this long fight, as well as persistent in adhering to the national security principles that can both win and secure the peace. Joe

Returning, I look down the hill at the destruction and immediately helped establish a Navy crisis response center nearby. I arrived home in the early morning hours, and upon returning again, I was directed by the Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) to establish the Navy's anti-terrorism unit, Deep Blue. A few days later, the CNO took the single power point slide I gave him to the "tank," where the Joint Chiefs of Staff were meeting with then-Secretary of Defense Rumsfeld. My slide cautioned that this new war with terror would last "at least a decade." My assignment took me to Afghanistan early in the conflict. There for a brief mission, I met up with SEALS late at night, watched detainees brought in....and learned that the illiteracy rate of women in Afghanistan was 98%. I then commanded a carrier battle group for the air strikes into the country - and later Iraq. As General Powell said of Iraq, "if you break it, you own it." And we broke it. Today, I often reflect upon my time at the National Security Council at the White House overseeing the Clinton's Administration's annual update of our nation's National Security Strategy of Engagement, laying out how to secure America's peace and prosperity, protect our interests abroad and at home, and grow our values - without entanglement. This week's horrific terror strike in Brussels, Belgium, highlighted once again that this war on terror is on a timeline far beyond what I even imagined when I wrote the original time frame for our military leaders. But, if followed with greater adherence, the national security principles of what we must do to defeat the threat of international terrorism - including ISIS - remain resilient to win the war as well as to secure an enduring peace. First, in a global war, no nation stands alone. I saw this firsthand after 9/11, when I commanded a battle group in the Persian Gulf. I had 30 ships to carry out our mission - but only 10 of them were American ships. We were a coalition, and success was defined by our ability to work seamlessly with foreign militaries. It's how we won the Cold War, with over 60 defense arrangements with other nations, as well as U.S. leadership of international diplomatic and economic institutions. This need is starkly apparent today. We must work more closely with our global allies to share intelligence, support police actions against terror suspects, run bombing raids, and conduct joint on-the-ground operations to capture and kill ISIS members in Syria and in homelands. In an increasingly complex world, this requires occasionally working with those who are adversarial to us, such as Russia and Iran. Unscrupulous nations often liaise with unscrupulous elements. But when these adversarial nations are aligned against a common threat - ISIS - we need the intelligence on and from their elements, and their arms aimed at our shared enemy. Second, and in conjunction, diplomacy matters. Look through the Wiki-leaks and see how resourceful in intelligence gathering, alliance building, spying, and advancing U.S. interests the State Department is. The downgrading of their manpower and resources has hindered our efforts at a time when information sharing is our most-needed asset. Diplomacy isn't meant to be a cup of tea. It is often intended to "hurt" if the diplomatic overture isn't accepted (e.g., Iranian and Iraqi sanctions). Diplomacy is used to benefit another nation's interest - if we gain by doing so. So let President Assad remain in Syria (for now) if his supporters (Iran and Russia) then pivot to focus on the common enemy, ISIS. In return, we restrain the more moderate anti-Assad militias with Saudi and Jordanian support, corralling together other Arab nations, with disjointed "partners" from Iran and Russia, to Turkey and Iraq. Diplomacy must work even with the devil....just don't sin. Third, economics is the bedrock of power - ours and our adversaries'. It's why China wants to dominate the South China Sea: to secure the undersea resources that it needs for economic power projection. And it's why the ISIS caliphate must be denied the approximate $2 billion in annual revenue flow from oil field profits to taxes, ransom to theft. Our airpower must more aggressively kick the legs out from under these economic support systems that fuel ISIS' foreign policy (such as destroying oil fields and not just oil tankers) and cutting off internal funding by destroying the infrastructure that collects/extorts taxes from the population. Our Treasury and international banking institutions we lead need to close down the remaining money to choke ISIS to death. Fourth, the new domain of warfare is cyberspace. We must dominate this new area of warfare as we do any other, so that we know what ISIS is thinking, trying and doing. With troubling questions about the competence of Belgium's and other allies' intelligence and law enforcement services, we must enhance our support for their efforts in order to protect ourselves. The right metric of warfare today is knowledge gained by sensors, human intelligence and the network, and our capability to quickly turn this gained information into swift action. Measuring what it takes to win our conflicts requires leadership that shifts the procurement benchmark away from capacity in numbers of ships, brigades, air wings, and toward capability in acquiring knowledge. But it also takes leadership to address a resolvable conflict between national security, civil rights and proprietary rights. Appropriate safeguards that also permit access to dangerous information that flows through the internet and into consumer phones must be established. Whether it's Apple's proprietary rights or our citizens' civil rights on the internet, resolution of the proper balance cannot be permitted to wait for the next crisis. Resolve them now ... or our security may be breached before we do. Finally, don't just win the war, but secure the peace - or, as General Powell said, we will still "own" the aftermath of unresolved conflict in the Middle East. Military strategists cautioned centuries ago that, in war, leaders must not "take the first step without considering the last." U.S. strategy to defeat ISIS needs an explanation of how we want our military and diplomatic efforts to end. For once the Caliphate ends, where do remaining ISIS forces lurk off to? Who fills the cracks left by the Caliphate's breakage? How is Syria rebuilt after 9 million of its people have been displaced, 250,000 killed, and more maimed? What of the replacement of basic services and infrastructure in the region, from homes to rudimentary sanitary and water services? What conflict do these open areas provide fertile ground for? What is the ending we want with the demise of ISIS that precludes us from having to return? Simply, although imperfect, it is a peace that provides us stability, which likely means Russia's continued influence in Syria; Iran with its limited role in the outcome; Sunnis and Shias separated if they cannot peacefully co-exist; and the Kurds no more restive than a semi-autonomous state. That is why the 98% illiteracy rate of Afghani women I learned about when on the ground at the beginning of the war struck me most: resolving that issue would do more to secure the peace than most else. After all, while our military stopped the problem of a fascist Germany, it took the Marshall Plan of economic recovery to secure the peace by a democratic Germany with economic security. As I reminisce on the time from the plane striking the Pentagon on 9/11 to today's effort to stop ISIS - an outgrowth of Al-Qaida in Iraq that arose after our invasion of Iraq - I acknowledge that the fog of war means no strategy is perfect. But today, the fog can be lifted by better utilization of the light that cyberspace can shine; the intelligence sharing that other nations and groups can provide; a diplomacy that binds others to our common interests; and a strategy that has as it's ultimate objective not only winning, but securing the peace for our security and prosperity. As John F. Kennedy said, "The hour is late, but the agenda is long," and we must be persistent in this long fight, as well as persistent in adhering to the national security principles that can both win and secure the peace. Joe | "" "" "" | ---|---|--- | Friends of Joe Sestak P.O. Box 1936 Media, PA 19063 | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | "" |

name: Joe Sestak
subject: DSCC - power corrupts, but DC power corrupts absolutely
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194f7/3476a20e/c7eb7c8/27459a6d/507382513/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

Peter, Today, I was in a press conference outlining "The Strategy for ISIS." As former head of the Navy's Anti-Terrorism Unit; as the most senior military officer ever elected to Congress; groomed during operational and war commands, as well as by national security assignments under leaders from the Chairman of the Joints Chiefs, General Colin Powell, to President Clinton on his National Security Council in the White House, I uniquely offer our nation a Senator with more than just the decades-long national security experience that is so sorely absent in today's Senate, or in anyone running for office in our nation. I offer what is missing in today's Democratic Senators (and Republican Senators): a keen understanding from the breadth and depth of my experience that militaries can stop a problem - they just can never fix a problem. Democratic Senators are still in the Senate who voted for Iraq, including our two Democratic leaders - and I doubt they believe now that our military fixed that country. And I singularly offer this nation a Senator with the credibility to convince others of the rightness of an understanding of the proper use of our military. **Yet, just as I entered today 's press conference on national security in how to best advance our nation's - our people's - interest, I was informed that these same Democratic Senators who hold the political power in DC had the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) endorse my primary opponent - even as (or because) - we lead my opponent by 16 points, and are within the margin of error of beating Sen. Toomey, with my opponent three times more behind. ** _What most concerns me is not the DSCC endorsement. The people of Pennsylvania know that there are no more kings and there are no more kingmakers in America, anymore - there is only "We the People."_ | [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194f7/3476a20e/c7eb7c8/27459a6c/507382513/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0323FBb1")
---
_What concerns me is the corruption of power __-__ rather than leadership __-__ by these DC power holders on the road to their unusual endorsement._ **They even forced an organization of patriotic Americans who always provided independent support for me in every campaign to step aside. At the same time, the Senators said millions of their own available resources would be used against me and PA 's people.**
Somehow, what this campaign began with a year ago - that I most wanted to regain the trust of the American people in Pennsylvania that had been lost by those in power positions (especially in DC) - is only more of a rallying call, today ... even in my own party, not just Trump's.
**I ask you to help me show the people of Pennsylvania what Martin Luther once said, "Here I stand, I can do no other," for people, not power, not politics.**
[Please contribute today as much as you can, or might for I believe it is still individual contributions of people like you that will prevail over the millions that my party's DC power holders have said they would put in against us.](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194f7/3476a20e/c7eb7c8/27459a6b/507382513/VEsF/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0323FBp1")
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194f7/3476a20e/c7eb7c8/27459a6a/507382513/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0323FBb2")
---
Warmly,
Joe

](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/17194f7/3476a20e/c7eb7c8/27459a68/507382513/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/admiraljoesestak-web?refcode=Footer")

No.

I’ve faced bullies before, and I’m not afraid to stand up to this one. If he wants to keep demonizing Mexican Americans and Muslim people in a cynical play for votes, we'll keep calling him out. name: BernieSanders.com
body:
#
We need to talk about the importance of Saturday, sisters and brothers.
Washington (101 delegates) -- Hawaii (25 delegates) -- Alaska (16 delegates)
We have a chance to do very well in these states. In Washington alone, the largest of the states voting this weekend, 35,000 people have showed up at just three of the last rallies for Bernie. And when you pair our potential for success with our victories on Monday and Tuesday, **we could cut into Hillary Clinton 's delegate lead by more than 15 percent by the end of the week.**
But we simply must do well this weekend, and that's why we have to ask:
[**Contribute $3 to our campaign today and we can win the delegates we need to continue on our path toward victory in the Democratic primary. Your contribution today could be the difference for an additional delegate this weekend.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/our-path-to-victory?refcode=em160323-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/our-path-to-victory?refcode=em160323-full")
We are still underdogs in this campaign, as we have been from the start. But so are many of the working families who have given so much of themselves in support of our campaign. No one ever said taking on the political, financial, and media establishment in this country would be easy, but if we continue to stand together, we can win.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

subject: I'm not surprised

body:
Our opponent was endorsed by ANOTHER big business group. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41fd/1c469400/596c722/351a398/4161038536/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/endorse-coc?utm_source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&utm_campaign=coc-rj&utm_medium=ema") Friend -- Senator Ron Johnson was just endorsed by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce, and I'm not at all surprised. This is the same U.S. Chamber of Commerce that:
* Opposes the _Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act_ \-- which helps women fight for equal pay in the workplace

* Called the minimum wage "counterproductive," a "distraction," and said that it "doesn't help"

* Supported free trade deals with South Korea, Colombia, and Panama

* Has made ramming the dangerous Trans-Pacific Partnership through Congress a top priority

**[Senator Johnson can rake in all the big money, corporate support he can fit into a super PAC -- and we'll keep adding more names to our list of grassroots supporters. Add your name to endorse Russ today.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41fd/1c469400/596c722/351a398/4161038536/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/endorse-coc?utm_source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&utm_campaign=coc-rj&utm_medium=ema")** Instead of listening to the people of Wisconsin, Senator Johnson is out auditioning to haul in more national corporate money to bail out his campaign. **Add this to Senator Johnson's two super PACs, and it's clear that he's outsourcing his campaign to outside groups with their millions in corporate funds.** What matters to our campaign is genuine grassroots support from the hardworking people across this country who work day in and day out. We need to send a loud, clear message to Senator Johnson and his corporate and Wall Street funders: **Our people beat your cash every time.** Our campaign is about helping middle class and working families get ahead -- it's not about peddling to the interests of billionaires and corporations. **[Help send a message to Senator Ron Johnson and the U.S. Chamber of Commerce by telling them you're on Team Russ today, tomorrow, and on Election Day.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41fd/1c469400/596c722/351a398/4161038536/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/endorse-coc?utm_source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&source=ema_2016-03-23_coc-rj_ns&utm_campaign=coc-rj&utm_medium=ema")** Thanks for being a part of our people-powered campaign. Michael Tyler

clean_body: Peter, Did you see the debate last week? There was absolutely no doubt that Eric was the clear winner! It. Wasn't. Even. Close. Watch these two clips below and read so you can see for yourself how well Eric did. You'll notice the career politicians continue to come after him, but he stood strong against their ridiculous attacks. Later in the debate, while the politicians waffled about their potential support for tax increases, Eric stepped in to make his position clear: we don't need to raise taxes. Period. I tell you what -- I'm more excited than ever because I know, that with your help, we're going to win. Look here, the main thing is to make sure the main thing is the main thing, and the main thing is winning. Our team is focused and working hard every day -- we need you to come out and work with us. Let's win this thing together! You'll have the time of your life (and eat plenty of Chick-Fil-A) -- I guarantee it. As always, let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for standing with us on this mission. On to Victory! Friends -- One year ago today, **I announced my run for U.S. Senate.**

And six years ago today, President Obama signed the Affordable Care Act into law. With 20 million Americans and two million Floridians gaining health insurance, it embodies why I want to serve as your Senator: to improve the lives of everyday people.

But we knew this wouldn't be easy. In fact, Republican Super PACs and the RNC are already readying big-money attacks against me. [**Will you stand with me and contribute to win back this Senate seat? We have just $3,254 left to raise tonight to meet our goal.**](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae236d/738133e4/3476a0dc/7f821680/345667149/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160323?refcode=pme160323&recurring=true&amount=25.00")

Marco Rubio abandoned the Senate, and now his run for President too. He left his seat wide open -- our race has just been named one of the _most likely to flip_ in the country this year.

**Let 's make the most of this important opportunity. **[**Pitch in now to help us turn Marco Rubio 's seat blue and meet our goal by midnight.**](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae236d/738133e4/3476a0dc/7f821680/345667149/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160323?refcode=pme160323&recurring=true&amount=25.00")

Thanks for being a part of this,

Patrick Murphy
